ddls:
CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the economic indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the economic indicator */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or area the economic indicator is applicable to */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the economic indicator data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the economic indicator was recorded */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the economic indicator (e.g., percentage, dollars) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geographical_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geographical feature */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the geographical feature */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the geographical feature */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the geographical feature */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or area the geographical feature is applicable to */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the geographical feature data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the geographical feature was recorded */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the geographical feature (e.g., meters, feet) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_prices" (
  "price_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each price record */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the price record */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the property */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the historical price data */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used to determine the price (e.g., market, appraisal) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the price record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("price_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., agent, investor, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the user is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the user is located */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code where the user is located */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Machine learning algorithm used */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value of the property */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the prediction */,
  "source_data" TEXT /* Source of the data used for the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., valuation, investment) */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property the report is about */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the property */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the property was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the property */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
